title: JAX-Sen: Placenta Collection, Processing, and Shipment Protocol for Spatial Transcriptomics
author: Alicia Warren

steps.title: 10% NBF fixative x PBS Paraffin...
steps.instruction: 10% NBF fixative x PBS Paraffin Tweezers Appropriate container for fixing

steps.title: The tissue specimen should be always...
steps.instruction: The tissue specimen should be always kept at 4 degrees Celsius and RNase-free. It is crucial to not store the tissue specimen at RT to avoid any cell death, and tissue and/or RNA degradation. After embedding, protect from FFPE blocks from light when possible.

steps.title: Collection:
steps.instruction: Collection:

steps.title: Euthanize mice via cervical dislocation a
steps.instruction: Euthanize mice via cervical dislocation a. Record start time

steps.title: Open the female's abdomen carefully as...
steps.instruction: Open the female's abdomen carefully as to not damage any fetuses. a. Euthanize fetuses via decapitation if they are older than E15

steps.title: Remove the hind left limb from one animal per litter
steps.instruction: Remove the hind left limb from one animal per litter.

steps.title: Keep in 1x PBS until ready...
steps.instruction: Keep in 1x PBS until ready to take a image for limb staging

steps.title: Separate individual yolk sac containing fetus and embryos
steps.instruction: Separate individual yolk sac containing fetus and embryos

steps.title: Remove any un-needed tissue (such as...
steps.instruction: Remove any un-needed tissue (such as yolk sac and uterine wall) from placenta. There will be a layer of tissue on the side on the placenta connecting to the umbilical cord that will need to be removed. Similar looking layer of tissue on the other side is part of the placenta and should not be removed.

steps.title: Take appropriate measurements in mm a
steps.instruction: Take appropriate measurements in mm a. Diameter of the placenta b. Crown to rump length (CRL) of embryo

steps.title: 10% NBF
steps.instruction: 10% NBF: place placenta is scintillation vial filled with FFPE (10% NBF). Ensure that the placenta is completely submerged

steps.title: Transfer to a shaker at room...
steps.instruction: Transfer to a shaker at room temperature to fix for 24 hours.

steps.title: Take piece of the embryo (ideally...
steps.instruction: Take piece of the embryo (ideally tail) for genotyping and another piece as back-up. Place on wet ice.

steps.title: Clean tools and proceed to next animal
steps.instruction: Clean tools and proceed to next animal

steps.title: Record time when the last sample was collected
steps.instruction: Record time when the last sample was collected

steps.title: Fixation, embedding, and sectioning:
steps.instruction: Fixation, embedding, and sectioning:

steps.title: After 24-hours of fixation in 10%...
steps.instruction: After 24-hours of fixation in 10% NBF, transfer the sample into a labelled cassette

steps.title: Place cassettes into 1x PBS
steps.instruction: Place cassettes into 1x PBS. Keep cold at 4C and RNase-free

steps.title: Embed samples in paraffin and trim to expose tissue
steps.instruction: Embed samples in paraffin and trim to expose tissue. a. Place the placenta (disc-shaped) upright, and not flat. See picture below.

steps.title: When sectioning, take 5um-thick slices
steps.instruction: When sectioning, take 5um-thick slices.

steps.title: Shipping:
steps.instruction: Shipping:

steps.title: Seal samples and ship to Jackson...
steps.instruction: Seal samples and ship to Jackson Laboratory for Genomic Medicine, Farmington, CT 06032 on cold packs to keep samples at 4C
